text: Refactoring is the process of improving the structure of existing code without changing what it does. The goal is to make the code cleaner, more readable, and easier to maintain. For example, a programmer might rename unclear variables, split long functions into smaller ones, or remove duplicate logic. These changes do not alter the software's behavior or add new features. Over time, code can become messy and difficult to work with—this is known as technical debt. Regular refactoring helps pay off this debt, reducing bugs and saving time in the long run. Good refactoring makes the codebase more understandable for the whole team.
